name: Ogou
shortDescription: Ogou is a powerful and complex spirit (loa) in Vodou traditions, associated with iron, war, fire, and justice. He is a fierce protector and a symbol of strength and courage.
description: Ogou is a prominent loa in Haitian Vodou and other related African diasporic religions. He is a warrior spirit, often depicted as a soldier or blacksmith, embodying strength, courage, and leadership. His domain encompasses iron, metalworking, war, fire, and justice. Ogou is seen as a powerful protector, fiercely loyal to his followers and a staunch defender of truth. He is invoked for strength in times of adversity, for protection from enemies, and for justice to be served. Different aspects of Ogou exist, each with distinct characteristics and roles. Ogou Feray is the most commonly known, associated with iron and blacksmithing. Ogou Badagris is linked to the sea and navigation, and Ogou Balendjo is often associated with healing. His colors are typically red and blue. Offerings to Ogou often include rum, cigars, and iron objects. He is a complex figure, representing both the destructive power of war and the constructive force of creation and justice. He requires respect and proper veneration, reflecting the seriousness of his power and influence within the Vodou pantheon. He serves as a bridge between the human and spirit worlds, offering guidance and assistance to those who seek his aid. He is a reminder of the importance of courage, strength, and justice in the face of adversity.

sources.title: Divine Horsemen: The Living Gods of Haiti
sources.author: Maya Deren
